purpose: Primitive operations below layer kernels. Layer kernels are folded into architecture-specific op-trees by composing these primitives; primitives must be benchmarked before they are trusted inside a full kernel.

source_research.name: Arm Compute Library
source_research.lesson: Operator performance is selected from architecture-specific algorithm families and tuned kernels, not one generic layer loop.

source_research.name: gemmlowp
source_research.lesson: Low-precision GEMM requires both an optimized kernel and companion packing code; architecture-specific optimized paths use intrinsics or assembly.

source_research.name: XNNPACK
source_research.lesson: High-efficiency inference is organized around operator-specific microkernels selected for target architectures.

source_research.name: Arm ACLE Neon intrinsics
source_research.lesson: Intrinsics expose SIMD instructions, but the useful abstraction is still packing plus fixed-shape microkernels.

dyadic_value_model.kernel_rule: Accumulate activation * signed_odd in registers, then apply scale and bias at the output-channel boundary.

primitive_classes.name: pack_weight_k_nr
primitive_classes.role: Reorder dyadic weights into K-major, NR-lane order for a target microkernel.

architecture_notes.arm64_neon: NR must match vector lane strategy, usually 4 or 8 float lanes; avoid building lane vectors from row-strided loads inside the hot loop.
architecture_notes.arm64_sme: NR should map to tile columns; packing should feed SME tile loads when available.
architecture_notes.x86_avx2: NR should map to 8 float lanes or paired vectors.
architecture_notes.x86_avx512: NR should map to 16 float lanes or tile-AMX variants for integer paths.

primitive_classes.name: pack_activation_mr_k
primitive_classes.role: Copy or alias MR activation rows into a contiguous K-major tile.

architecture_notes.arm64_neon: MR must be large enough to reuse packed weights across rows but small enough to keep accumulators in vector registers.
architecture_notes.all: For GEMV, this primitive is skipped; row-dot is cheaper.

primitive_classes.name: make_indirect_window_tile
primitive_classes.role: Create an MR tile of convolution windows without fully materializing im2col.

architecture_notes.arm64_neon: Prefer contiguous interior windows and separate border kernels; avoid LD2/deinterleave-heavy direct loops unless benchmarked.
architecture_notes.all: This primitive is the conv equivalent of activation packing for GEMM.

primitive_classes.name: dot_f32_i16
primitive_classes.role: Single-row dyadic dot primitive for GEMV and small tails.

architecture_notes.arm64_neon: Use vector int16 load, sign-extend, convert to float, FMLA, horizontal add.
architecture_notes.all: Only valid as a performance primitive when subkernel gate beats materialized row-dot.

primitive_classes.name: microkernel_mr_nr_f32_i16
primitive_classes.role: Core dyop GEMM primitive: compute MR rows by NR output channels.

architecture_notes.arm64_neon: Candidate shapes must be benchmarked, e.g. MR=2/4 and NR=4/8. Reuse activation scalar/vector across NR, and reuse packed weight lanes across MR.
architecture_notes.arm64_sme: Candidate should map MRxNR directly to SME tiles.
architecture_notes.x86_avx2: Use NR=8 or split into two 4-lane accumulators depending on conversion cost.
architecture_notes.x86_avx512: Use NR=16 and consider int16-to-float conversion throughput.

primitive_classes.name: apply_scale_bias
primitive_classes.role: Apply dyadic scale and optional bias to accumulator tile.

architecture_notes.all: Fuse after accumulation at output-channel boundary; do not decode weights to float memory.

primitive_classes.name: writeback_strided
primitive_classes.role: Store output tile to the layer output layout.

architecture_notes.all: Linear can write contiguous rows; Conv2d NCHW usually needs plane-stride stores unless output layout changes.

architecture_notes.arm64_neon: Vectorize contiguous chunks; persistent worker overhead must be avoided for tiny tensors.
architecture_notes.all: Prefer fusing into producer/consumer op-tree when possible.

primitive_classes.name: reduction_window
primitive_classes.role: Pooling/reduction primitive for maxpool and average-pool tails.

architecture_notes.all: Dedicated tiny-shape kernels are required when worker overhead dominates.

mandatory_primitives: pack_weight_k_nr mapped to SME tile columns
pack_activation_mr_k mapped to SME tile rows
microkernel_mr_nr_f32_i16 or integer dyop variant using SME tile operations
